user.title: FortiGate
user.description: Use a REST API administrator token (System → Administrators). Minimum scope: read access to System and Network for monitoring; read-write on interface objects if you enable interface switches in options.

data.port: HTTPS port (default 443; custom admin port e.g. 8443 is common)
data.access_token: API token (shown once when the API admin is created)
data.vdom: VDOM name (use root for single-VDOM appliances)

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the FortiGate (host, port, firewall, or TLS).
error.invalid_auth: Authentication failed. Check the token and that the admin profile allows REST read access.
error.api_error: The FortiGate responded but the payload was unexpected (wrong VDOM, firmware, or API path).
error.unknown: Unexpected error, see Home Assistant logs.

init.title: FortiGate options
init.description: Interfaces are discovered from the firewall. "All" skips common internal FortiOS names (ssl.*, naf.*). Use "Only selected" to limit entities. Enabling administrative switches can disconnect your network or management access.

data.interface_mode: Which interfaces get sensors, binary sensors, and optional switches
data.tracked_interfaces: Interfaces to include when "Only selected" is chosen
data.enable_interface_switches: Expose CMDB switches to bring interfaces administratively up/down (requires write permission)
data.enable_sdwan_sensor: Poll SD-WAN health endpoint (no extra entities if SD-WAN is not configured)

error.no_interfaces_selected: Choose at least one interface when using "Only selected", or switch back to "All".
